item.title: Woo Hoo! I passed the million Updoots mark this weekend!
item.description:
So, I'm just supposed to contact the admins, and trade these points in for a new Bugatti Divo, right? (EDIT - I forgot to say thank you my Dudes, for all of the updoots.)
I can't wait to drive it! Maybe if everyone who sees this post updoots it, I can get 2 million points by this afternoon and then redeem those for the Divo that has the factory fog lights and truck nuts. EDIT EDIT - Most Viral? Click here for my reply https://imgur.com/a/VmSlTgz
item.dc:creator: imflukeskywalker

item.title: Apparently this is how fish are artificially created in farms...
item.description:
As stated in comments, artificially fertilized, not created.
I saw it on Reddit and decided if I had to endure it then so do y'all. A little interesting though. Edit: yay front page, I certainly won't say no to fun pictures, especially on RHM!

item.title: The smallest opera house in Italy, 99 spectators, but with perfect acoustics
item.description: Teatro Donnafugata, built in the 19th century under a palazzo in Ragusa, Sicily. It now has an entrance from the outside. https://www.tv2000.it/retroscena/2019/05/02/donnafugata-il-piu-piccolo-teatro-sposato-alla-scala/ http://www.teatrodonnafugata.it/

item.title: They are mass murderers
item.description: I really, really hope Zełenski will deliver on his promise. Everybody personally involved in shelling cities and murdering fleeing civilians deserves nothing else but death. The West should stop holding back. Deliver any weapon possible, help training recruits, hit Russia with digital war, stop just reacting, counterattack. https://www.theguardian.com/world/2022/mar/07/ukraine-volodymr-zelenskiy-vows-revenge-russia-forces-fleeing-family-civilians-killed-shelling-irpin-town
item.dc:creator: CollinRubinstein

item.title: Brainwashed
item.dc:creator: roaduardo

item.title: Russia has placed mines on humanitarian corridors. JFC
item.description: Refugees trying to escape the besieged Ukrainian city of Mariupol using so-called humanitarian corridors were left stranded as the road they were directed towards was mined, the International Committee of the Red Cross (ICRC) has said. Dominik Stillhart, director of operations at the ICRC, urged both military forces to come to an immediate agreement on the exact routes and times available for those seeking safe passage out of the country. He told BBC Radio: So far we have seen, unfortunately, only agreements in principle. But they have immediately broken down because they lack precision. They lack the kind of … agreements over times, over roads, over whether people can go out or goods can come in. To just give you an idea, we have a team in Mariupol on the ground. They were ready yesterday [Sunday] despite the fact that it was not entirely clear what exactly the agreement was. As soon as they reached the first checkpoint, they realised that the road that was indicated to them was actually mined. Efforts to get people out of Mariupol - the scene of ferocious fighting - collapsed for a second day running on Sunday with both sides accusing each other of breaching a ceasefire agreement. (Via AFP)
item.dc:creator: TheN8

item.title: Don't change Glasgow
item.description: For those confused, that cone is both a Glasgow tradition and something of a rite of passage for young weegies. https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Equestrian_statue_of_the_Duke_of_Wellington,_Glasgow F/B Edit: Please donate to the Red Cross appeal for Humanitarian Aid in Ukraine if you can https://donate.redcross.org.uk/appeal/ukraine-crisis-appeal
item.dc:creator: KalWilson
